forecast-period.text: A cold front over the east of South Australia will move clear of the state by midday today. A second cold front will move across the west this afternoon reaching central and eastern parts early Friday morning. A cooler, fresh southerly flow following the second front will gradually ease as a high pressure system drifts in from the west for the weekend.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.

area.description: Far West Coast: WA-SA Border to Fowlers Bay

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Thursday for Far West Coast
forecast-period.text: Southwesterly 25 to 30 knots.
2.5 to 3 metres.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2.5 metres, increasing to 2 to 4 metres around midday.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm from the late morning.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Friday for Far West Coast
forecast-period.text: Southwesterly 20 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots early in the morning. Winds turning southerly 15 to 25 knots in the early afternoon.
1.5 to 2.5 metres.
Southwesterly 3 to 4 metres, decreasing to 3 metres around midday.
Partly cloudy. 95% chance of showers.
South to southeasterly 10 to 15 knots tending east to southeasterly below 10 knots during the day.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing below 1 metre during the morning.
South to southwesterly 2 to 3 metres.
Southerly below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the afternoon or evening.
Cloudy.

area.description: Upper West Coast: Fowlers Bay to Elliston

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Thursday for Upper West Coast
forecast-period.text: Northwesterly 25 to 30 knots turning west to southwesterly in the late morning and early afternoon.
2 to 3 metres.
Westerly 1 to 2 metres, tending southwesterly 2 to 3 metres during the afternoon.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm from the late morning.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Friday for Upper West Coast
forecast-period.text: Southwesterly 20 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots early in the morning. Winds turning southerly 15 to 25 knots in the middle of the day.
1.5 to 2.5 metres.
Southwesterly 3 to 4 metres, decreasing to 3 metres during the afternoon.
Partly cloudy. 95% chance of showers.
Southerly 15 to 20 knots turning southeasterly 10 to 15 knots during the morning.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing below 1 metre during the afternoon.
Southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres, tending southerly 2.5 to 3 metres during the evening.
Partly cloudy. 60% chance of showers.

area.description: Lower West Coast: Elliston to Cape Carnot

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Thursday for Lower West Coast
forecast-period.text: Northwesterly 25 to 30 knots turning southwesterly in the late afternoon.
2 to 4 metres.
West to southwesterly 1 to 2 metres inshore, increasing to 2 to 3 metres offshore by early evening.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the late morning and afternoon.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Friday for Lower West Coast
forecast-period.text: Southwesterly 20 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots offshore early in the morning. Winds turning southerly in the middle of the day.
1.5 to 2.5 metres.
Southwesterly 3 to 4 metres, decreasing to 3 metres around midday.
Partly cloudy. 95% chance of showers.
Southerly 15 to 20 knots turning southeasterly 10 to 15 knots during the morning.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing to 1 metre during the morning.
Southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres.
Southeast to southwesterly around 1 metre, tending south to southwesterly 1 to 2 metres during the afternoon or evening.
Cloudy. 60% chance of showers.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Thursday for Central Coast
forecast-period.text: North to northwesterly 25 to 30 knots shifting southwesterly 20 to 30 knots in the evening.
2 to 3 metres.
Southwesterly 1 to 1.5 metres, increasing to 1.5 to 2 metres in the west.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the late morning and afternoon.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Friday for Central Coast
forecast-period.text: Southwesterly 20 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots early in the morning. Winds turning southerly in the early afternoon.
1.5 to 2.5 metres.
Southwesterly 2 to 4 metres, decreasing to 3 metres during the afternoon.
Partly cloudy. 95% chance of showers.
Southerly 15 to 20 knots turning southeasterly 10 to 15 knots during the day.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing to 1 metre during the afternoon.
Southwesterly 2 to 3 metres.
Below 0.5 metres, tending southerly 1 to 2 metres during the morning.
Partly cloudy. 70% chance of showers.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Thursday for South Central Coast
forecast-period.text: Northwesterly 20 to 30 knots turning west to southwesterly during the afternoon and evening.
2 to 2.5 metres.
Southwesterly 1.5 metres.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the late morning and afternoon.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Friday for South Central Coast
forecast-period.text: Southwesterly 20 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots early in the morning. Winds turning southerly in the early afternoon.
1.5 to 2.5 metres.
Southwesterly 1.5 metres, increasing to 2 to 3 metres during the morning.
Partly cloudy. 95% chance of showers.
Southerly 15 to 20 knots.
1.5 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1 metre during the afternoon.
South to southwesterly 3 to 4 metres, decreasing to 2 to 3 metres during the afternoon.
Below 0.5 metres, tending southeast to southwesterly 1 to 2 metres during the morning.
Cloudy. 80% chance of showers.

area.description: Upper South East Coast: Murray Mouth to Cape Jaffa

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Thursday for Upper South East Coast
forecast-period.text: Northwesterly 20 to 30 knots.
1.5 metres, increasing to 2 to 2.5 metres around midday.
Southwesterly 1 to 1.5 metres.
Partly cloudy. 90% chance of showers.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Friday for Upper South East Coast
forecast-period.text: Westerly 20 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots offshore early in the morning. Winds turning south to southwesterly 15 to 25 knots early in the morning.
1.5 to 2.5 metres.
Southwesterly 1.5 metres, increasing to 2 to 3 metres around midday.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers.
Southerly 15 to 20 knots turning southeasterly 10 to 15 knots during the day.
1 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1 metre during the afternoon.
South to southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres.
South to southwesterly around 1 metre.
Partly cloudy. 80% chance of showers.

area.description: Lower South East Coast: Cape Jaffa to SA-VIC Border

forecast-period.warning-summary: Gale Warning for Thursday for Lower South East Coast
forecast-period.text: Northwesterly 20 to 30 knots, reaching up to 35 knots south of Beachport in the late evening.
1.5 to 2 metres, increasing to 2.5 to 3 metres during the afternoon.
Southwesterly 1.5 metres.
Partly cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the afternoon and evening.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Gale Warning for Friday for Lower South East Coast
forecast-period.text: West to northwesterly 25 to 30 knots, reaching up to 35 knots south of Port MacDonnell during the morning. Winds turning south to southwesterly 20 to 30 knots during the morning.
2 to 3 metres.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2 metres, increasing to 2 to 3 metres during the morning, then increasing to 3 to 4 metres during the afternoon.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers.
Southerly 15 to 25 knots decreasing to 10 to 15 knots during the morning.
1.5 to 2.5 metres, decreasing to 1 metre during the afternoon.
Southerly 2.5 to 3 metres inshore, increasing to 3 to 4 metres offshore.
Partly cloudy. 80% chance of showers.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Thursday for Spencer Gulf
forecast-period.text: North to northwesterly 20 to 30 knots tending west to northwesterly in the evening.
1.5 to 2.5 metres.
South to southwesterly around 1 metre.
Becoming cloudy. 80% chance of showers south of Cowell to Wallaroo, 50% chance elsewhere.
Northwest to southwesterly 20 to 25 knots becoming southwesterly 15 to 25 knots before dawn then tending southerly 15 to 20 knots in the evening.
1.5 to 2.5 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the morning.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 2 metres during the morning.
Partly cloudy. 95% chance of showers.
Southerly 15 to 20 knots turning southeasterly 10 to 15 knots during the evening.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing to 1 metre during the afternoon.
South to southwesterly around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 2 metres south of Cowell to Wallaroo.
Partly cloudy. 70% chance of showers.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Thursday for Gulf St Vincent
forecast-period.text: North to northwesterly 20 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots during the afternoon and evening.
1.5 to 2.5 metres.
West to southwesterly below 0.5 metres.
Becoming cloudy. 80% chance of showers.
Westerly 15 to 20 knots, reaching up to 25 knots north of Port Vincent to Brighton early in the morning. Winds turning south to southwesterly during the day.
1 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres around midday.
West to southwesterly below 0.5 metres.
Partly cloudy. 95% chance of showers.
Southerly 15 to 20 knots turning southeasterly below 10 knots during the day.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing below 1 metre during the afternoon.
Southeast to southwesterly below 0.5 metres.
Partly cloudy. 80% chance of showers.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Thursday for Investigator Strait
forecast-period.text: North to northwesterly 20 to 30 knots turning westerly 20 to 25 knots in the evening.
1.5 to 2.5 metres.
West to southwesterly below 1 metre.
Partly cloudy. 90% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the afternoon.
Southwesterly 15 to 25 knots turning southerly 15 to 20 knots in the late afternoon.
1.5 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the morning.
West to southwesterly around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the morning, then decreasing to around 1 metre later in the evening.
Partly cloudy. 95% chance of showers.
South to southeasterly 15 to 20 knots decreasing to about 10 knots during the evening.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing below 1 metre during the afternoon.
West to southwesterly around 1 metre, increasing to 1.5 to 2.5 metres through the Backstairs Passage.
Partly cloudy. 70% chance of showers.
